userStories.title: Extract _rebuild_regime_rank_cache helper to close DRY violation
userStories.description: HMMEngine._label_regimes() and HMMEngine.load() both inline the same np.lexsort((np.arange(len(regime_returns)), regime_returns)) over self.model.means_[:, 0] and the same {int(rid): rank for rank, rid in enumerate(sorted_ids)} dict construction. Extract a private helper _rebuild_regime_rank_cache(self) -> np.ndarray that sets self._sorted_regime_ids and self._regime_id_to_rank and returns sorted_ids. Call it from the head of _label_regimes() (using the returned sorted_ids for the label loop and dropping the tail cache assignment) and from load() (replacing the inline lexsort block). Do NOT call _label_regimes() from load() — that would clobber the 5 RegimeInfo fields restored from pickle.
acceptanceCriteria: HMMEngine._rebuild_regime_rank_cache exists, raises RuntimeError when self.model is None, and returns the sorted_ids numpy array
_label_regimes() calls self._rebuild_regime_rank_cache() — no inline np.lexsort tail assignment remains
load() calls engine._rebuild_regime_rank_cache() — no inline np.lexsort block remains in the load path
grep for 'np.lexsort' in shared/core/hmm/hmm_engine.py returns exactly one match (inside the helper)

New regression test in tests/hmm/test_hmm_engine.py asserts RegimeInfo fields probability, recommended_strategy_type, max_leverage_allowed, max_position_size_pct, min_confidence_to_act round-trip through save/load with non-default values intact
uv run ruff check shared/core/hmm tests/hmm exits 0
uv run ruff format --check shared/core/hmm tests/hmm exits 0
uv run ty check shared/core/hmm tests/hmm reports no in-scope errors
uv run python -m pytest tests/hmm -q shows all tests passing (53+ tests)
